upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The graph/TU upgrade is attempted, but the slice graph and worked dual certificate have load-bearing algebra/sign errors, so the computation delta is not yet valid.

repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 has only a one-cutoff retained-plan interval; no many-neighborhood laminar flow certificate

published_prior_art.cite: schrijver_1986_theory_linear_integer + orlin_1993_mincostflow: standard TU/network-flow machinery once the RD LP is a genuine network

repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 is one-cutoff; this claims a simultaneous many-neighborhood sharp envelope

published_prior_art.cite: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds is sharp one-cutoff manipulation RD; Bertanha 2020 and Cattaneo et al. 2021 are multi-threshold/multi-cutoff point-ID, not manipulation partial-ID flow envelopes

repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 has no node-potential graph dual

published_prior_art.cite: orlin_1993_mincostflow and schrijver_1986_theory_linear_integer cover standard min-cost-flow dual/TU certificates once the network formulation is established

repo_prior_art.cite: parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 has no J=2 many-neighborhood worked flow certificate

published_prior_art.cite: none as causal prior art; it is a worked instance of standard min-cost-flow duality

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation rubric is only partially delivered: the proposal gives an explicit graph/exhibit, but the fixed-slice equivalence and dual certificate are not algebraically valid as stated.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship sharp envelope over M_ext is advertised as the constructive object, but §9 computes only one fixed lambda,m slice, not the global lower/upper envelope from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison cites GRR 2020, Bertanha 2020, and Cattaneo et al. 2021 by bibkey only, with no section/equation/page anchor for the precise open gap.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The LP carry-flow equations and graph balances do not match: §6 omits the j=1 carry-out term, introduces z_{Jw} without a corresponding meaningful terminal balance, and the graph can permit flows not represented by the displayed LP.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed dual certificate contradicts its own complementary-slackness rule: f(s,u22)<u(s,u22) but sigma_(s,u22)=1, and the source-arc inequality arithmetic uses +sigma although the stated inequality has -sigma.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Fix the network convention end to end: choose one node-balance sign, include the correct carry terms for every layer, and restate Theorem 1 with a bijection between LP variables and arcs.
Recompute Exhibit 9.2 from the corrected dual program and report every finite-capacity arc's dual inequality, slack sign, and complementary-slackness status.
Add a §9 exhibit that actually computes B_L/B_U or a support-function value after optimizing over a nontrivial M_ext, not just a fixed-slice flow.
Add location-level anchors to GRR 2020 and Rosenman et al. 2019 for the exact gap: one-cutoff sharp manipulation bounds versus discrete optimized bounds without a laminar TU certificate.

recommended_next_step: Do not send this version to Stage 0; repair the graph/dual algebra and add a global-envelope exhibit before re-review.
